channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump looks to turn attention to Western Hemisphere at Americas summit
item.description: President Trump is set to gather with Latin American leaders on Saturday at his Miami-area golf club as his administration looks to turn attention to the Western Hemisphere, at least for a moment.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump is set to gather with Latin American leaders on Saturday at his Miami-area golf club as his administration looks to turn attention to the Western Hemisphere, at least for a moment.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump administration's embattled FDA vaccine chief is leaving for the second time
item.description: The FDA's controversial vaccine chief, Dr. Vinay Prasad, is leaving the agency. It's the second time he has abruptly departed following decisions involving the review of vaccinations and specialty drugs.
item.content:encoded:
The FDA's controversial vaccine chief, Dr. Vinay Prasad, is leaving the agency. It's the second time he has abruptly departed following decisions involving the review of vaccinations and specialty drugs.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Family, former presidents and a Hall of Famer give Rev. Jesse Jackson a final sendoff
item.description: Several speakers at Jackson's funeral invoked his hallmark catchphrases: "Keep hope alive" and "I am somebody."
item.content:encoded:
Several speakers at Jackson's funeral invoked his hallmark catchphrases: "Keep hope alive" and "I am somebody."

(Image credit: Erin Hooley)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Bernard LaFayette, Selma voting rights organizer, dies at 85
item.description: Bernard LaFayette, who died Thursday, laid the foundations of the Selma, Alabama, campaign that culminated in the passage of the Voting Rights Act. He was a Freedom Rider and helped found the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee.
item.content:encoded:
Bernard LaFayette, who died Thursday, laid the foundations of the Selma, Alabama, campaign that culminated in the passage of the Voting Rights Act. He was a Freedom Rider and helped found the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee.

(Image credit: Gregory Smith)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Netflix acquires Ben Affleck's AI company
item.description: Affleck's company helps filmmakers build their own AI models that take care of time-intensive details.
item.content:encoded:
'/>

Affleck's company helps filmmakers build their own AI models that take care of time-intensive details.

(Image credit: Clive Mason)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Oil surges to its highest price since 2023, and stocks drop after U.S. jobs report
item.description: Oil shot to its highest price since 2023 after surging again because of the Iran war, and a weak update on the U.S. job market knocked stocks lower to cap Wall Street's worst week since October.
item.content:encoded:
Oil shot to its highest price since 2023 after surging again because of the Iran war, and a weak update on the U.S. job market knocked stocks lower to cap Wall Street's worst week since October.

(Image credit: Seth Wenig)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: No lawsuits required: U.S. Customs is working on a system to refund tariffs
item.description: U.S. Customs told the trade court it aims for a streamlined process in 45 days to return importers' money without requiring individual lawsuits.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Customs told the trade court it aims for a streamlined process in 45 days to return importers' money without requiring individual lawsuits.

(Image credit: Ted S. Warren)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Middle East conflicts largely avoided energy facilities in the past. Not in this war
item.description: Recent conflicts in the region have either spared energy infrastructure or caused limited damage. That isn't the case in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.
item.content:encoded:
Recent conflicts in the region have either spared energy infrastructure or caused limited damage. That isn't the case in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.

(Image credit: Maxar)
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon

item.title: Poll: A majority of Americans opposes U.S. military action in Iran
item.description: Most Americans disapprove of President Trump's handling of Iran, and a majority sees Iran as either only a minor threat or no threat at all, an NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds.
item.content:encoded:
Most Americans disapprove of President Trump's handling of Iran, and a majority sees Iran as either only a minor threat or no threat at all, an NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds.

(Image credit: Sohrab)
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: Your Winter Paralympics primer: What, who and how to watch
item.description: Hundreds of para athletes are competing in Italy through March 15. Many Americans are defending past titles, with the U.S. sled hockey team hoping to fend off rival Canada for its fifth straight gold.
item.content:encoded:
Hundreds of para athletes are competing in Italy through March 15. Many Americans are defending past titles, with the U.S. sled hockey team hoping to fend off rival Canada for its fifth straight gold.

(Image credit: Mattia Ozbot)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman
